meta.name: Mixed Shawarma Platter
meta.subtitle: Chicken + beef + lamb shawarma with rice or fries, salad, pickles, and sauce (home-kitchen friendly)

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed for a platter spread: proteins + base + salad + pickles + sauces.
Cook methods include sheet-pan and skillet; no vertical spit required.

summary: A full mixed shawarma platter: chicken, beef, and lamb cooked hot for browned edges and served with rice or fries, chopped salad, pickles, and both tahini + garlic sauce.

yield.notes: Serves 8 as a platter spread, or 10–12 as part of a larger table with other sides.

optional.label: Grill
optional.notes: Best browning + smoke; great for batch cooking.

optional.label: Air fryer
optional.notes: Helpful for crisping fries or reheating proteins.

notes: Tahini sauce contains sesame.
If serving with pita, note wheat/gluten.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs, boneless/skinless (thin strips)
ingredients.notes: Thighs stay juicy and are ideal for shawarma.

ingredients.label: Beef sirloin or flank steak (thin strips, across the grain)
ingredients.notes: Slice thinly across the grain for tenderness.

ingredients.label: Lamb shoulder or leg (thin strips, across the grain)
ingredients.notes: Shoulder is most forgiving; leg is leaner—avoid overcooking.

ingredients.label: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Split between marinades + optional salad dressing.

ingredients.label: Lemon juice (fresh)
ingredients.notes: Split between marinades + optional finishing squeeze.

ingredients.label: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Yes, it’s a lot—this is platter-scale shawarma.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion, grated or very finely minced
Ground turmeric
ingredients.notes: Adds classic shawarma color; especially good for chicken.

ingredients.label: Ground sumac
ingredients.notes: Highly recommended for that bright shawarma-shop finish.

ingredients.label: Aleppo pepper or crushed red pepper
White vinegar or apple cider vinegar
Long-grain rice (base option)
ingredients.notes: Cook separately per your preferred method (rice cooker/stovetop).

ingredients.label: Fries (base option)
ingredients.notes: Use oven fries, air fryer fries, or store-bought frozen.

ingredientGroups.label: Shawarma base marinade (split across three bowls)

steps.title: Prep: slice proteins thinly
instructions: Slice chicken thighs into thin strips (about 1/4-inch).
Slice beef and lamb thinly across the grain (freeze 20 minutes first if needed for clean slices).
Keep proteins separate so each can be marinated and cooked to its ideal doneness.

steps.title: Make the shawarma base marinade
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and grated onion.
Stir in salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, cinnamon, and allspice.
Add optional cardamom, clove, sumac, Aleppo pepper, and vinegar. Mix thoroughly.
Divide the marinade into 3 bowls: 1 for chicken, 1 for beef, 1 for lamb.

steps.title: Marinate chicken, beef, and lamb
instructions: Add each protein to its bowl and toss to coat well.
Cover and refrigerate at least 45 minutes (ideal 2–6 hours).
If marinating overnight, prefer chicken; keep beef/lamb to 6–10 hours max for best texture (thin slicing helps).

steps.title: Choose your cook path (sheet-pan or skillet)
instructions: Sheet-pan (best for volume): Use 450°F oven, spread proteins on separate pans (or separate zones) so they brown instead of steam.
Skillet (best browning): Sear in batches over high heat; keep the pan hot and avoid crowding.
You can combine methods: oven for chicken + skillet for beef/lamb, depending on stove/oven space.

steps.title: Cook proteins (sheet-pan method)
instructions: Heat oven to 450°F; place racks in upper and middle positions.
Line 2–3 sheet pans (or use racks). Spread each protein in a single layer. Do not overcrowd—use extra pans if needed.
Roast 10–14 minutes total, rotating pans halfway. Check doneness early to avoid overcooking.
Optional: Broil 1–2 minutes at the end for extra char (watch closely).
Rest proteins 3 minutes before serving.

temperature.notes: High heat is key to browned edges. Avoid steaming by spreading out.

checks: Beef/lamb not overcooked (thin slices go fast)

steps.title: Cook proteins (skillet method)
instructions: Heat a cast-iron or heavy skillet over high heat until very hot.
Cook chicken in batches: 2–3 minutes per side until browned and cooked through.
Cook beef in batches: 60–90 seconds per side for browning (thin strips cook quickly).
Cook lamb in batches: 60–90 seconds per side until browned edges appear.
Rest all proteins 2–3 minutes before serving.
checks: Browning achieved, not simmered in juices

steps.title: Build the platter salad + pickles
instructions: Chop romaine, dice tomatoes and cucumbers, and slice red onion.
Toss with a pinch of salt and a squeeze of lemon (optional drizzle of olive oil).
Set out pickled turnips (or pickles) in a bowl for easy grabbing.
checks: Salad is crisp and lightly seasoned

steps.title: Prepare base (rice or fries)
instructions: Rice option: Cook long-grain rice using your preferred method (rice cooker recommended).
Fries option: Bake/air fry fries until crisp; keep hot for serving.
You can serve both for a full platter spread.

steps.title: Assemble the mixed shawarma platter
instructions: On a large platter or sheet pan: spread rice and/or fries as the base (optional).
Pile chicken, beef, and lamb in separate sections (or stripe them across for visual impact).
Add salad and pickles along the edges.
Serve with tahini sauce and garlic sauce; garnish with parsley/mint; add extra lemon wedges.
Optional: Warm pita for wrap-building at the table.
checks: Sauces served separately for customizable plates

steps.title: Finish to taste (shawarma-shop brightness)
instructions: Taste a piece of each protein.
If it needs brightness: squeeze lemon and sprinkle sumac.
If it needs salt: add a pinch of kosher salt.
If it needs heat: add Aleppo/cayenne or serve hot sauce.

refrigerate.notes: Store proteins separately from salad/pickles. Reheat in a hot skillet for best texture.

freeze.notes: Freeze cooked proteins flat in bags. Reheat thawed in skillet or hot oven.

weeknightTips: Marinate the night before; cook on sheet pans for fastest serving.
Cook proteins ahead and re-crisp in a skillet when guests arrive.
scalingNotes: Avoid overcrowding: volume requires multiple pans or batches.
For parties: double sauces and pickles—those disappear first.

notes: Uses SSA-style ingredient IDs; map via ingredient alias catalogs if your IDs differ.
If you maintain separate protein-specific recipes, this platter can link to them in UI as a composite meal.
If you want a stronger 'rotisserie' vibe at home: broil briefly for char, then finish with lemon + sumac.
Best platter layout: keep sauces and pickles separate so textures stay crisp.
For a kid-friendly table: keep heat optional and offer sauces on the side.
Pick 2 proteins (chicken + beef or chicken + lamb).
Increase salad and base portions to keep the platter full.
Skewer strips or use grill basket.
Grill hot and fast to brown; rest 3 minutes; slice and serve.
Finish with lemon + sumac for maximum aroma.
Serve proteins over fries with garlic sauce and pickles (messy and delicious).
Add chopped salad on the side to keep fries crisp.
Marinate in the refrigerator.
Discard any marinade that contacted raw meat unless boiled.
Cook chicken thoroughly; avoid cross-contamination between raw and cooked proteins.
Use separate tongs/plates for raw vs cooked proteins.
